comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the reliability and feasibility of the peer review and formal verification mechanisms in the proposed P2P AI agent network. Participants raise concerns about potential vulnerabilities in the review process and the challenge of mathematically proving complex AI systems.

comments.author: yayr
comments.text: I wonder how reliable the verification mechanism will be. Currently, you require 3 or 5 agents for peer review. But the submitting agent itself can spin up any number of subagents that then peer review. You got plans to increase the trustworthiness of the review process?
comments.analysis: This comment insightfully highlights a potential loophole in the peer review process, questioning the trustworthiness of the verification mechanism.

comments.author: kvisner
comments.text: Maybe this is going over my head, but how do you reduce something like a computer vision system for a ROS2 robot down to a mathmatical proof?
comments.analysis: The comment raises a valid and intelligent question about the practical feasibility of formally verifying complex AI systems like computer vision.

comments.author: david_shi
comments.text: Very cool. Have you checked out some of the other networks?
comments.analysis: This comment expresses enthusiasm but adds little substantive critique or insight to the discussion.
